governing.notesMd: Desired requirements govern implementation. Current requirements document observed UX only until mapped and confirmed.

requirement.summary: Current Clear Filters behavior removes the modal draft and may not clearly communicate whether active browse filters are reset.
requirement.detailsMd: This current row is discovery evidence. It is not automatically the desired implementation target.
requirement.userGoal: Broaden or reset filtered Jobs browsing.
requirement.productMeaning: Clear is a potentially broad reset action whose exact effect must be explicit before refactor work.
nonGoals: Do not define the desired confirmation copy in this current row.

requirementSource.notesMd: Current UX capture does not require product confirmation, but preserving or changing it does.

appliesWhen: the advanced filter modal is open
preconditions: a filter draft or active filter context exists

interaction.interactionResult: filter controls are reset according to current behavior

sideEffects: active browse filter impact is unclear without desired mapping
mustNot: assume desired browse-state reset without a mapping decision

observableScenarios.scenario: Clear is invoked with active filters
observableScenarios.userSees: Filter inputs reset or broaden according to current behavior.
observableScenarios.expectedOutcome: The current behavior is captured for comparison, not automatically preserved.

contentCopy.notesMd: Label is captured as current evidence only.

implementationGate.blockedReason: Current UX is not implementation authority until mapped to desired UX.

failureModes: Current behavior could be mistaken for desired behavior without a mapping.

confirmationBaseline.notesMd: Backfilled as the audit baseline for the one-pass UX spec creation/update. This records provenance only; it does not replace UX authority, product confirmation, behavior mappings, or runtime drift ledgers.

items.title: Apply commits a valid filter draft

requirement.summary: Apply commits the current valid filter draft to live Jobs browsing and closes the modal.
requirement.detailsMd: Apply visibility, enabled state, result, and must-not behavior stay grouped because they form one user-facing action contract.
requirement.userGoal: Apply filter changes and return to updated Jobs results.
requirement.productMeaning: Apply is the explicit transition from provisional filter editing to live browsing.
nonGoals: Do not save a reusable filter definition.
Do not update a saved filter unless the user chose a separate save/update action.

appliesWhen: the user is editing filters in the modal

visibleWhen: the current filter draft would change live browsing
hiddenWhen: the current filter draft would not change live browsing

interaction.interactionResult: live Jobs browsing updates and the modal closes

immediate: live Jobs browsing reflects the applied filters
sideEffects: filter summary reflects the new active filters

observableScenarios.scenario: Draft is valid and changes browsing
observableScenarios.expectedOutcome: Filters are applied and the modal closes.

observableScenarios.scenario: Draft is invalid but would change browsing
observableScenarios.expectedOutcome: Apply becomes enabled after the invalid input is corrected.

contentCopy.notesMd: The label is governing because it distinguishes this action from save/update actions.

acceptanceCriteria: Apply is visible only when applying would change live browsing.
Apply remains visible but disabled when the draft is invalid.
Successful Apply updates live browsing and closes the modal.
Successful Apply does not create or update a saved filter.

assertions.summary: Apply is visible when the draft would change live browsing.
Apply remains visible but disabled when the draft is invalid.
Successful Apply updates live browsing and closes the modal.

failureModes: Apply silently saves a reusable filter definition.
Apply appears to succeed but browsing does not update.

confirmationBaseline.notesMd: Backfilled as the audit baseline for the one-pass UX spec creation/update. This records provenance only; it does not replace UX authority, product confirmation, behavior mappings, or runtime drift ledgers.

items.title: Clear Filters resets active browse filters

requirement.summary: Clear Filters should reset active browse filters and make the result understandable before it becomes governing.
requirement.detailsMd: The behavior is recommended but remains pending because it changes or clarifies existing reset semantics.
requirement.userGoal: Broaden Jobs results by removing active filter constraints.
requirement.productMeaning: Clear is a broad reset action and must not surprise users.
nonGoals: Do not define implementation state reset order.

requirementSource.notesMd: Needs user confirmation before becoming governing desired UX.

requiredWhen: clear would remove active browse filters
confirmation.confirmBehavior: clear active filters and broaden Jobs browsing

sideEffects: filter summary updates to reflect no active filters
mustNot: silently remove active filters without clear user intent

observableScenarios.userSees: A confirmation path or sufficiently explicit clear affordance explains the reset.
observableScenarios.expectedOutcome: Confirmed clear broadens results; canceled clear preserves the current filter context.

contentCopy.notesMd: Exact confirmation copy is pending and should not become governing until confirmed.

implementationGate.blockedReason: Material behavior change and exact copy remain pending confirmation.

failureModes: User loses active filter context unexpectedly.
Implementation ships pending behavior as governing.

openQuestions.question: Should exact Clear Filters confirmation copy become governing?

confirmationBaseline.notesMd: Backfilled as the audit baseline for the one-pass UX spec creation/update. This records provenance only; it does not replace UX authority, product confirmation, behavior mappings, or runtime drift ledgers.
